title: Multi-thread depth on large deals doubled since Q1
anchor: Active opportunities above $250K ACV average 4.2 engaged contacts in Q2, up from 2.1 contacts in Q1.
connect: Win rate at 4+ contacts runs at 41% versus 18% for deals with 1-2 contacts.
body: Active opportunities above $250K ACV average 4.2 engaged contacts in Q2, up from 2.1 contacts in Q1. Win rate at 4+ contacts runs at 41% versus 18% for deals with 1-2 contacts.
trace: Multi-thread depth improvement on $250K+ deals shows clear correlation with win rate improvement, suggesting better buying committee engagement.

title: Champion re-engagement moves deals forward within three weeks
anchor: 9 of 14 deals advanced a stage within 21 days of champion re-engagement events in April.
connect: Champion documentation rate crossed 50% for the first time in Q2 at 51%.
body: 9 of 14 deals advanced a stage within 21 days of champion re-engagement events in April. Champion documentation rate crossed 50% for the first time in Q2 at 51%.
trace: Champion re-engagement correlation with stage movement suggests champion touch timing is a key deal velocity lever.

title: Q3 pipeline coverage sits at 3.4x with strong activity levels
anchor: Open Q3 pipeline runs $4.2M versus the $1.24M quota aggregate, with $3.3M showing live engagement in the last 14 days.
connect: Single-threaded coverage share dropped to 22% in Q2 from 34% in Q1.
body: Open Q3 pipeline runs $4.2M versus the $1.24M quota aggregate, with $3.3M showing live engagement in the last 14 days. Single-threaded coverage share dropped to 22% in Q2 from 34% in Q1.
trace: Coverage looks healthy with good engagement rates and improving multi-thread discipline across the book.

title: Proposal stage compression accelerates with reissue timing
anchor: 11 of 14 aged proposal-stage deals advanced or closed in the last 30 days, with 6 of those 11 moving within 5 days of a re-issued proposal.
connect: Enterprise Proposal-to-Closed-Won median sits at 38 days in Q2 versus 49 days in Q1.
body: 11 of 14 aged proposal-stage deals advanced or closed in the last 30 days, with 6 of those 11 moving within 5 days of a re-issued proposal. Enterprise Proposal-to-Closed-Won median sits at 38 days in Q2 versus 49 days in Q1.
trace: Proposal reissue timing appears to be a strong velocity lever, with clear compression in enterprise deal cycles.

title: Mid-market win rate climbs to 27% with ICP alignment driving lift
anchor: Q2 mid-market win rate sits at 27.1% compared to 19.1% in Q1, with ICP-aligned deals accounting for 89% of the improvement.
connect: Champions documented in CRM correlate with 38% win rate versus 21% without champion identification.
body: Q2 mid-market win rate sits at 27.1% compared to 19.1% in Q1, with ICP-aligned deals accounting for 89% of the improvement. Champions documented in CRM correlate with 38% win rate versus 21% without champion identification.
trace: Win rate improvement appears driven by better ICP qualification and champion identification rather than broad execution improvements.

title: Beacon head-to-head win rate jumps to 61% in Q2
anchor: Head-to-head deals versus Beacon Systems show 11-7 record of 18 total for 61.1% win rate in Q2.
connect: Trailing four quarters Beacon head-to-head win rate ran at 24%.
body: Head-to-head deals versus Beacon Systems show 11-7 record of 18 total for 61.1% win rate in Q2. Trailing four quarters Beacon head-to-head win rate ran at 24%.
trace: Dramatic improvement in Beacon head-to-head performance suggests positioning or execution changes are working.

title: Beacon battlecard utilization climbs alongside win rate improvement
anchor: Beacon Systems battlecard utilization in active head-to-head deals sits at 72% in April, up from 31% in February.
connect: Head-to-head win rate on Beacon deals climbed 14 points in the same window as utilization increase.
body: Beacon Systems battlecard utilization in active head-to-head deals sits at 72% in April, up from 31% in February. Head-to-head win rate on Beacon deals climbed 14 points in the same window as utilization increase.
trace: Clear correlation between battlecard usage and win rate improvement suggests content effectiveness in competitive situations.

title: Meridian AI presence doubles in active deals post-funding
anchor: Meridian AI appears in 26% of Q2 competitive opportunities versus 10% in Q1, with 7 of 12 Meridian deals reaching negotiation within 30 days of their Series B announcement.
connect: Meridian's $25M Series B was announced March 14, 2026.
body: Meridian AI appears in 26% of Q2 competitive opportunities versus 10% in Q1, with 7 of 12 Meridian deals reaching negotiation within 30 days of their Series B announcement. Meridian's $25M Series B was announced March 14, 2026.
trace: Meridian's funding event correlates with increased competitive presence and faster deal progression in their pipeline.

title: Sterling enterprise deal expands buying committee post-executive demo
anchor: Sterling enterprise opportunity grew from 4 to 7 engaged contacts following the April 18 executive demo, adding CFO and two business-unit leads.
connect: Historical close rate for committees that expand during evaluation runs 26% above committees that stay flat.
body: Sterling enterprise opportunity grew from 4 to 7 engaged contacts following the April 18 executive demo, adding CFO and two business-unit leads. Historical close rate for committees that expand during evaluation runs 26% above committees that stay flat.
trace: Sterling deal shows positive committee expansion signal with executive engagement driving additional stakeholder involvement.

title: Revised outbound sequence lifts meeting conversion to 14%
anchor: Outbound sequence-to-meeting conversion sits at 14% with the revised cadence launched April 5, booking 42 discovery meetings from 300 contacts.
connect: Prior sequence conversion rate ran at 9%.
body: Outbound sequence-to-meeting conversion sits at 14% with the revised cadence launched April 5, booking 42 discovery meetings from 300 contacts. Prior sequence conversion rate ran at 9%.
trace: Outbound sequence revision shows measurable improvement in meeting booking rates within three weeks of deployment.

title: Enterprise closes concentrate in high-thread deals

connect: 18 of 22 active enterprise opportunities above $250K ACV show 4+ engaged contacts in Q2.
body: 7 of 9 Q2 enterprise closes carried multi-thread depth of 5 or more engaged contacts. 18 of 22 active enterprise opportunities above $250K ACV show 4+ engaged contacts in Q2.
trace: Enterprise deal success pattern clearly correlates with deep multi-threading across both closed and active opportunities.

title: Customer expansion pipeline builds from Q2 onboarding completions

title: Outcome reason documentation improves after field launch

connect: Q1 capture rate sat at 64% versus Q2's 88% completion rate.
body: 19 of 23 deals with full activity capture completed after the April 5 outcome reason field launch. Q1 capture rate sat at 64% versus Q2's 88% completion rate.
trace: System enhancement for outcome tracking correlates with significant improvement in deal documentation completeness.

title: Beacon competitive presence increases to 29% of opportunities

connect: Beacon head-to-head win rate improvement coincides with their increased deal presence.

title: Pipeline engagement rate maintains at 79% across open opportunities

title: Inbound-to-meeting conversion holds steady at 31%

connect: Outbound sequence revision lifted meeting conversion from 9% to 14% in the same period.
